purpose: Local-first project memory QA layer for LLM agents.

choices.description: Multiple trusted agents share confirmed project knowledge.
One agent gets its own isolated Vault project.
A customer, team, or business domain gets a separate Vault project.
Throwaway Vault project for demos, tests, and benchmarks.

common_install_architecture: ask setup language for manual CLI installs; non-interactive agents can pass --language
ask whether local stdio MCP should be configured
ask whether optional semantic search and embedding dependencies should be enabled
ask whether optional Supabase sync/read dependencies should be enabled
ask whether optional Headroom context compression is needed for long logs or tool output
ask whether Profile / Dream / Forgetting memory-agent guidance should be generated
ask whether developer/benchmark dependencies should be enabled
ask whether user profile/persona memory should stay private by default, with only reviewed summaries shared
if optional features are selected, ask whether to install their Python dependencies now
if semantic search is selected, ask whether to download and configure a local ONNX embedding model
if Supabase sync is selected, start with simple setup guidance and ask whether to generate daily cron, LaunchAgent, or n8n sync templates
ask whether to connect an existing Obsidian vault
run first Obsidian dry-run/import when requested
ask whether to schedule automatic Obsidian sync
agent_install_prompt: Install Vault-for-LLM for this project. Read AGENTS.md and agent_manifest.json, ask whether the vault should be shared or private, ask setup language for manual CLI installs, ask separately about MCP, semantic search, Supabase sync, Headroom context compression, Profile / Dream / Forgetting memory-agent guidance, and dev/benchmark dependencies, ask whether user profile/persona memory should stay private by default with only reviewed summaries shared, install selected optional dependencies when confirmed, ask whether semantic should download a local ONNX embedding model, ask whether an existing Obsidian vault should be imported, configure CLI/MCP, run the first Obsidian dry-run/import if requested, ask whether automatic sync should be scheduled, and run a search/read/propose smoke test.

agent_setup_wizard.non_interactive_example: vault setup-agent --non-interactive --agent codex --scope shared --agent-project-dir /path/to/project --features core,mcp,obsidian_import --obsidian-vault /path/to/ObsidianVault --import-obsidian --obsidian-sync all
agent_setup_wizard.non_interactive_with_optional_deps: vault setup-agent --non-interactive --agent codex --scope shared --agent-project-dir /path/to/project --features core,mcp,semantic,supabase,headroom --language en --install-optional-deps --install-embedding-model mix --supabase-setup simple --supabase-sync cron --json

optional_features.prompt: Install the core local Markdown + SQLite + keyword search workflow?
optional_features.when_to_install: Always. This is the minimal local-first Vault workflow.
optional_features.notes: No cloud service or embedding model is required.

optional_features.prompt: Configure local stdio MCP tools for this agent?
optional_features.when_to_install: Use when the runtime supports MCP, such as Hermes Agent, Codex surfaces with MCP, OpenCode, Claude Code, OpenClaw generic MCP, or other MCP hosts.
optional_features.notes: vault-mcp is a local stdio server. Do not expose it as an unauthenticated public network service.

optional_features.prompt: Do you already have an Obsidian vault to import into this Vault project? If yes, provide its path.
optional_features.when_to_install: Use when the user already keeps project notes in Obsidian and wants agents to search them through Vault-for-LLM.
install_commands: vault setup-agent --non-interactive --agent generic --scope private --agent-project-dir /path/to/project --features core,mcp,obsidian_import --obsidian-vault /path/to/ObsidianVault --obsidian-sync all
post_install: Ask whether to schedule this same --compile command with cron, LaunchAgent, n8n, or the host agent for automatic sync.
optional_features.notes: The importer writes to raw/obsidian/ and skips .obsidian, .trash, .git, and 00-Vault-Knowledge by default. Always run --dry-run first, then perform the first --compile import only after the user confirms the Obsidian path.

optional_features.prompt: Install semantic search and embedding workflow dependencies?
optional_features.when_to_install: Use when the user wants embedding-backed semantic or hybrid retrieval and accepts larger optional dependencies.
install_commands: vault setup-agent --non-interactive --agent generic --scope shared --agent-project-dir /path/to/project --features core,mcp,semantic --install-optional-deps --install-embedding-model mix --json
optional_features.notes: Deterministic hash embeddings are only for smoke tests and require explicit --allow-hash.

optional_features.when_to_install: Use when a team needs remote read paths, a synced copy of local SQLite data, or cross-host memory sharing for agents on different machines.
install_commands: vault setup-agent --non-interactive --agent generic --scope shared --agent-project-dir /path/to/project --features core,mcp,supabase --language en --install-optional-deps --supabase-setup simple --supabase-sync cron --json
optional_features.notes: SQLite remains the source of truth. Supabase can provide a shared remote read/sync layer for different hosts. Start with --supabase-setup simple; use advanced only for RLS, multi-agent allow-lists, Coze/n8n read-only access, or sensitivity-based sharing. Do not sync full content unless the user explicitly wants --include-content. If privacy gates block files, clean the source notes before recompiling instead of bypassing the gate.

optional_features.prompt: Install optional Headroom context compression for long logs, tool output, or large retrieved context?
optional_features.when_to_install: Use only when the agent has context-window or token pressure after Vault has already narrowed retrieval to relevant source ranges.
post_install: Use Headroom after Vault retrieval only when the selected context is still too large.
Keep final citations tied to original vault_read_range output, not compressed summaries.
optional_features.notes: Headroom is an adjacent optional compression layer, not a Vault dependency and not the source of truth for citations.

optional_features.prompt: Generate Profile / Dream / Forgetting memory-agent guidance?
optional_features.when_to_install: Use when the user wants dedicated memory-maintenance agents, user-profile summaries, dream reports, or governed forgetting workflows.
install_commands: vault setup-agent --non-interactive --agent generic --scope shared --agent-project-dir /path/to/project --features core,mcp,memory_agents --language en --json
post_install: Keep Profile agents candidate-only by default.
Keep Dream / Forgetting agents report-only by default.
Do not promote, delete, or sync private profile memory without user-approved policy.
optional_features.notes: This feature writes guidance only. It does not install a model, start a scheduler, promote memory, or delete entries.

optional_features.when_to_install: Use for source checkouts, PR work, benchmark runs, and release validation.
optional_features.notes: Use source installs for repo modification and CI-style validation.

mcp_tool_profiles.notes: full remains the default for backward compatibility; production agents should prefer core to reduce MCP tool-schema tokens. Tool profiles are visibility filters, not access-control boundaries.

memory_write_policy.direct_active_writes: avoid in shared vaults unless the user explicitly approves

memory_governance.rule: L0-L3 describe memory depth and usage, not access control.
memory_governance.vision: Vault-for-LLM is the user-owned memory core; agent runtimes are hands; models are compute tools.
memory_governance.user_centered_design: Vault can store project knowledge and reviewed user profile memory. Raw private interactions stay private; trusted agents may share short reviewed summaries.
memory_governance.progressive_memory_disclosure: Start with boot summaries and active context, then topic maps, search candidates, bounded source ranges, and raw/archive memory only when task and permissions justify it.

read_filter.rule: For shared vaults, pass agent_id on reads. Use include_private only when the user or local policy allows that agent to read owner/allow-list private memory. Use max_sensitivity as a hard cap.

profile_guidance.L0: minimal identity only; private by default
profile_guidance.L1: durable work preferences and collaboration rules
profile_guidance.L2: recent state or care summaries; use expires_at
profile_guidance.L3_private: deep analysis and raw private interaction history

dedicated_memory_agents.profile_agent: Maintains stable user profile, preferences, care summaries, and agent-specific boundaries without exposing raw private chats.
dedicated_memory_agents.dream_forgetting_agent: Runs dream reports, deduplication, stale-memory review, promotion/archive suggestions, and expiry recommendations for low-value context.

memory_governance.sharing_rule: Share reviewed project knowledge and short summaries; keep raw private conversations, persona files, and high-sensitivity profile notes local unless the user explicitly approves wider sharing.
memory_governance.obsidian_rule: Preserve layer plus governance metadata in frontmatter; adding metadata is compatible with import/export.
memory_governance.supabase_rule: Use RLS with scope, sensitivity, owner_agent, allowed_agents, and status; do not use layer as a permission level.

setup_agent_non_interactive: vault setup-agent --non-interactive --agent codex --scope shared --agent-project-dir /path/to/project --features core,mcp,obsidian_import --obsidian-vault /path/to/ObsidianVault --import-obsidian --obsidian-sync all

supported_runtimes.name: Hermes Agent / Nancy
supported_runtimes.integration: stdio MCP plus optional scheduled CLI jobs

supported_runtimes.name: n8n
supported_runtimes.integration: CLI command node, internal HTTP wrapper, or MCP bridge

supported_runtimes.name: Codex
supported_runtimes.integration: CLI in workspace; MCP where supported

supported_runtimes.name: OpenCode
supported_runtimes.integration: generic stdio MCP where supported, or shell CLI commands

supported_runtimes.name: Claude Code
supported_runtimes.integration: stdio MCP server or shell CLI commands

safety_rules: Do not commit runtime vault.db files, report artifacts, benchmark outputs, or secrets.
Do not expose vault-mcp directly to the public internet.
Do not present benchmark percentages as universal scores without dataset and probe caveats.
For shared vaults, use candidate-first memory writes by default.
Do not rely on /tmp virtualenvs for scheduled agent jobs; use a stable path such as ~/.hermes/venvs/vault-for-llm/.
